name: Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel — circular from Daimiel
name_original: Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel — circular desde Daimiel
region_intro: Touring cycling around Daimiel is characterized by its unique natural landscape, primarily centered around the Tablas de Daimiel National Park. This region features a flat terrain, extensive lagoons, and reed beds, formed by the confluence of the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers. The area is a significant wetland ecosystem within the La Mancha plain, offering a distinctive environment for exploration. Many routes utilize wooden walkways that traverse the park, providing access to its diverse aquatic habitats. Best touring cycling routes around Daimiel The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel — circular from Daimiel, an 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the wetland ecosystem and its birdlife. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Molino de Melocho – Church of Peace (Daimiel) loop from Daimiel, an easy 18.5 miles (29.8 km) path. This route explores the flat agricultural landscapes surrounding Daimiel. Local touring cyclists also love the La Mancha Viewpoint – Church of Peace (Daimiel) loop from Daimiel, a 39.9 miles (64.3 km) trail leading through the broader La Mancha plain, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Daimiel is defined by flat plains, wetland ecosystems, and river floodplains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through the national park to more challenging routes across the wider region. The routes in Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 130 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Daimiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Daimiel?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Daimiel, offering a variety of options for exploring the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Daimiel?
A: The mild weather, especially during September and October, makes these months an enjoyable time for cyclists to explore the Daimiel region.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling around Daimiel?
A: The terrain around Daimiel is generally flat, characteristic of the La Mancha plain. Many routes traverse the unique wetland ecosystem of the Tablas de Daimiel National Park, featuring extensive lagoons, reed beds, and wooden walkways.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Daimiel offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052164195/parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-daimiel-circular-from-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel — circular from Daimiel&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 18.2-mile (29.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 44 minutes to complete, offering views of the wetland ecosystem and its birdlife.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Daimiel?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Daimiel are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052164195/parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-daimiel-circular-from-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel — circular from Daimiel&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive loop through the national park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in Daimiel?
A: While cycling, you can explore the unique wetland ecosystem of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt;, characterized by lagoons and reed beds. You'll also find various observation points, such as this &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation point&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3694805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecinos Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The park itself features well-defined routes like the Isla del Pan Itinerary with its wooden walkways.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on the touring cycling routes?
A: The Tablas de Daimiel National Park is a vital refuge for thousands of aquatic birds, making it an excellent spot for birdwatching. You can expect to see many migratory species, especially around the extensive lagoons and reed beds. Observation points are strategically located to enhance wildlife viewing.

Q: Are there specific viewpoints or observation points along the routes?
A: Yes, the Tablas de Daimiel National Park features numerous observation points and strategically located viewpoints. The Torre de Prado Ancho Itinerary, for example, culminates in a two-story tower offering panoramic views of the wetland. You can also find specific spots like this &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation point&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Daimiel?
A: The touring cycling routes around Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique natural landscape of the Tablas de Daimiel National Park, the flat terrain, and the opportunity to observe diverse birdlife.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes in Daimiel?
A: While many routes are easy, there are options for more experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mancha Viewpoint – Church of Peace (Daimiel) loop from Daimiel&lt;/a&gt; is a more difficult 39.9-mile (64.3 km) trail that leads through the broader La Mancha plain, typically completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: While the Tablas de Daimiel National Park is a protected area, some external routes around Daimiel may be suitable for cycling with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash. Always check specific park regulations and route guidelines before heading out.

Q: Is public transport available to access the touring cycling routes?
A: Information on direct public transport links to specific trailheads within the Tablas de Daimiel National Park is not readily available. Most visitors typically travel to Daimiel by car and then access the routes from there.
tips: Located at the entrance to the National Park, the “Molino de Molemocho” Visitor Center is an old hydraulic mill, like many others that used to grind grain in its day, taking advantage of the abundant flow of water. The mill is accessed through a pleasant walk of about 400 meters in length, along a wooden path whose beginning is in the car park set up for this purpose.

Las Tablas de Daimiel is a practically unique wetland in Europe and the last representative of the ecosystem called river tables, once characteristic of the central plain of our Peninsula. It is a complex ecosystem that mixes the characteristics of a floodplain, produced by the overflow of the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers at their confluence, with that of a groundwater discharge area from a large aquifer.
A beautiful gravel road of almost 7 km, practically flat, that takes us from the city of Daimiel to the National Park.

The history of this 17th century church is closely linked to the Order of Discalced Carmelites first and, later, to the Discalced Carmelites, an order founded by Saint Teresa of Jesus.
Its façade reflects the traditional Carmelite scheme of the Baroque. Its sober and simple interior is structured in a Latin cross plan and a single nave with a transept and a dome on pendentives.
